# `pricing.ron` ‚Äî field reference

<!-- GENERATED by `cargo run -p dc_data_docs` (dronecom #2815). Do not edit: field docs live as /// comments on the Rust structs. -->

Data: [`config/pricing.ron`](../config/pricing.ron)

## `PricingConfig`

The complete capability-pricing calibration, loaded from
`assets/config/pricing.ron` (#2634). It groups the reference engagement
envelope, the per-currency dollar rates, and the per-category tuning knobs
that the `price_chassis` / `price_sensor` / `price_warhead` functions read
to derive every part's cost at definition-load time.

### `envelope` : [`Envelope`](#envelope)

The reference engagement envelope every effect is saturated/normalized against.

### `rates` : [`CurrencyRates`](#currencyrates)

Dollar-per-point conversion rates, one per value currency.

### `sensor` : [`SensorKnobs`](#sensorknobs)

Sensor-pricing tuning knobs (also reused to shape chassis stealth premiums).

### `warhead` : [`WarheadKnobs`](#warheadknobs)

Warhead-pricing tuning knobs.

### `chassis` : [`ChassisKnobs`](#chassisknobs)

Chassis-pricing tuning knobs.

## `Envelope`

Reference engagement envelope: the named battle every price is
normalized against (design doc ¬ßCalibration).

### `map_radius_m` : f32

Playable-area radius (m). Detection footprint and operational radius
saturate here ‚Äî capability beyond the map is worthless.

### `reference_target` : [`ReferenceTarget`](#referencetarget)

The reference target/emitter every sensor footprint, the lethality
currency, and the hit-point currency are measured against.

### `raid_size` : u32

Typical raid size ‚Äî track/lock capacity saturates here.

### `reference_miss_distance_m` : f32

Reference terminal miss distance (m) ‚Äî fuze and blast credits saturate here.

### `engagement_timescale_secs` : f32

Reference engagement timescale (s) ‚Äî `scan_interval` is valued against it.

### `station_distance_m` : f32

One-way distance to a typical station (m) ‚Äî transit leg for availability.

### `reference_layer_depth_m` : f32

Standard thermocline depth (m) ‚Äî mirrors `PropagationToggles::standard()`'s 75 m; depth margin below this is what the depth premium credits.

### `deck_turnaround_secs` : f32

Deck cycle (recover‚Üírelaunch) time (s) ‚Äî availability denominator.

### `max_hp_pool` : f32

Largest HP pool in the game ‚Äî damage saturates here (overkill is free).

### `reference_payload_kg` : f32

Normalizers for the delivery/survivability currencies.

### `reference_speed_mps` : f32

Reference platform speed (m/s). The delivery currency's mobility factor
normalizes a hull's top speed against this (`mobility = (speed / reference_speed_mps)^exp`).

## `CurrencyRates`

Dollar rates: one per value currency (design doc ¬ßValue currencies).

### `em_footprint` : f32

$ per normalized EM footprint point (`footprint/map_area`, post-exponent).

### `acoustic_footprint` : f32

$ per normalized acoustic (sonar) footprint point ‚Äî the acoustic-domain
analogue of `em_footprint` (sonar sensors + acoustic quieting).

### `optical_footprint` : f32

$ per normalized IR/visual footprint point ‚Äî the optical-domain
analogue of `em_footprint` (EO/IR sensors + IR/visual signature reduction).

### `lethality` : f32

$ per normalized lethality point (damage/reference hp, post-exponent).

### `delivery` : f32

$ per normalized delivery point (payload √ó mobility √ó radius gate).

### `persistence` : f32

$ per unit of on-station availability (0..1).

### `hp` : f32

$ per normalized HP point (`hit_points / reference hp`).

### `battery_per_kwh` : f32

$ per kWh of battery capacity (the chassis battery term).

### `hull_per_kg` : f32

Manufacturing floor: $ per kg of hull weight (nothing prices to ~$0).

### `facility` : [`FacilityRates`](#facilityrates)

Nested per-slot facility rates for launch/recovery parallelism.

### `riders` : [`RiderRates`](#riderrates)

Flat riders (fixed $ credits) for discrete binary capabilities.

## `SensorKnobs`

Tuning knobs for the sensor value formula (`super::price_sensor`):
exponents, premiums, and quality multipliers. Some (`area_exponent`) are
reused to shape the chassis stealth premiums.

### `area_exponent` : f32

Sub-linear exponent on normalized footprint area (split test: two
dispersed radars beat one giant). Expect ~0.8.

### `lock_premium` : f32

Fire-control locks are worth this multiple of a search track.

### `scan_or_track_penalty` : f32

`TrackConcurrency::ScanOrTrack` multiplier (< 1; `WhileScanning = 1.0`).

### `refresh_exponent` : f32

Exponent on (`engagement_timescale / scan_interval`). Expect ~0.25.

### `refresh_clamp` : (f32, f32)

Clamp on the refresh multiplier, e.g. (0.5, 2.0).

### `bearing_only_quality` : f32

Product-quality multiplier for a passive bearing-only sensor (< 1:
single-platform passive can't be engaged without triangulation).

### `fdoa_quality` : f32

Product quality when fdoa: Capable restores passive ranging (‚â§ 1.0).

### `emitter_tax` : f32

Counter-detection multiplier when the sensor emits (RWR-visible), < 1.

## `WarheadKnobs`

Tuning knobs for the warhead value formula (`super::price_warhead`): the
damage exponent and the fuze/blast near-miss credit ceilings.

### `damage_exponent` : f32

Sub-linear exponent on normalized damage (two warheads = two chances
to leak through defences). Expect ~0.9.

### `fuze_credit_max` : f32

Max fuze credit multiplier at saturation (e.g. 0.5 ‚Üí up to 1.5√ó).

### `blast_credit_max` : f32

Max blast (near-miss damage retention) credit at saturation.

## `ChassisKnobs`

Tuning knobs for the chassis value formula (`super::price_chassis`):
speed exponents, the EM/acoustic/depth survivability premiums, and the
platform's share of horizon (vantage) value.

### `speed_exponent` : f32

Speed exponent for recoverable platforms (expect 1.0 ‚Äî split test neutral).

### `munition_speed_exponent` : f32

Speed exponent for expendable (munition-class) hulls ‚Äî the one designed
super-linearity (one fast missile beats two half-speed ones). Expect ~1.5.

### `em_stealth_premium_max` : f32

Max EM stealth premium, as a fraction of the hull's *other* subsystem
value: a fully-radar-invisible hull is worth this much more (stealth
protects the platform's own value ‚Äî #2634 calibration amendment,
decoupled from the sensor footprint rates).

### `acoustic_stealth_premium_max` : f32

Max acoustic stealth premium, same shape. Higher than EM: quieting is
the submarine's whole game.

### `vantage_utilization` : f32

Platform's share of horizon-unlocked sensing value.

### `depth_premium_max` : f32

Max premium (as a fraction of the hull's own subtotal) for a submarine's depth margin below the reference layer ‚Äî survivability, structured like the EM/acoustic stealth premiums.

### `depth_half_point_m` : f32

`saturating_credit` half-point (m) for the depth margin.

## `ReferenceTarget`

The target/emitter every sensor footprint is measured against.
Signature-as-strength convention matches `blueprint_stats` detection
matrices (radar‚Üírcs, sonar active‚Üíscs, sonar passive‚Üínoise, ‚Ä¶).

### `rcs` : f32

Radar cross-section of the reference target, in dimensionless
sim-signature units (signature-as-strength convention). A `Radar`
sensor's detection range is derived against this.

### `sonar_cross_section` : f32

Active-sonar cross-section, in sim-signature units. A `SonarActive`
sensor's detection range is derived against this.

### `passive_noise` : f32

Passive acoustic signature (radiated noise), in sim-signature units. A
`SonarPassive` sensor's detection range is derived against this.

### `ir` : f32

Infrared signature, in sim-signature units. An `Ir` sensor's detection
range is derived against this.

### `visual` : f32

Visual/optical signature, in sim-signature units. A `Visual` sensor's
detection range is derived against this.

### `emitter_strength` : f32

Reference emitter strength an RWR is valued against.

### `hp` : f32

Hit-point pool of the reference target. The normalizer (denominator)
for the lethality currency (warhead damage) and the hit-point currency
(chassis HP). Distinct from `Envelope::max_hp_pool`, which is where
overkill damage saturates.

### `altitude_m` : f32

Reference target operating altitude (m) for horizon-based terms ‚Äî a sea-skimmer; the target class a vantage matters against.

## `FacilityRates`

$ per parallelism slot, per facility kind.

### `flight_deck` : f32

$ per parallel flight-deck slot (fixed-wing launch/recovery on a carrier).

### `heli_pad` : f32

$ per parallel helicopter-pad slot (rotary-wing launch/recovery on a carrier).

### `well_deck` : f32

$ per parallel well-deck slot (surface-craft / USV launch & recovery on a carrier).

### `air_launch` : f32

$ per parallel air-launch rail (weapons launched from an Air-domain platform in flight).

### `vls` : f32

$ per parallel vertical-launch-system cell (Sea/Submarine platforms).

### `torpedo_tube` : f32

$ per parallel torpedo tube (Sea/Submarine platforms).

## `RiderRates`

Flat riders for discrete (binary) capabilities.

### `logistics_receiver` : f32

Chassis `carrier: true` ‚Äî can accept fuel/cargo logistics deliveries.

### `guidance_uplink` : f32

Chassis `guidance_uplink` present (not `GuidanceUplink::None`) ‚Äî a flat
credit for a mid-course guidance uplink datalink.

### `elevation` : f32

`SensorDef` elevation: Resolves (3D height-finding).

### `signature_capability` : f32

`SensorDef` `signature_capability` present (classification).

### `lock_detection` : f32

`SensorCapability` `lock_detection` flag (warns when locked).
